rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AFANASIA KIBIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No bank statements have been filed. [LRSF 14.35 D.] A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] The seller's closing statement shows total costs of $74,438.40. How were costs for the estate determined to be $33,325.00? Why did the estate pay the full amount of property taxes to the San Mateo County Tax Collector? Attorney costs paid on 2/16/19 and 4/8/19 are not supported (no itemization or invoice). Why was the Probate Referee paid another $550.50 on 4/9/19? Why was James Snidle paid another $500 to reappraise tangible personal property? The executor paid a total of $405.62 for gas for eight occurrences of "business-related travel" between 7/2/18 and 1/24/19 but also another $1,375 to Angel Sedan Services for travel to San Francisco between 9/18/18 and 1/21/19 for "Estate Work" without any explanation. It would have been helpful if the related case number had been cited somewhere... A beneficiary who has already received her bequest is no longer an interested party. The executor might wish to ask the attorney requesting special notice to withdraw his request. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/3/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BLYTHE ELIZABETH ERIKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/2021. Issues: 1) Please file a complete copy of the Nels Erikson First Party Special Needs Trust and any and all amendments. 2) Please explain who is the trustee of the Nels Erikson First Party Special Needs Trust. 3) The trustee of the Nels Erikson First Party Special Needs Trust has not received notice or waived an account. A trustee who waives the account of the personal representative must have filed a consent to act as trustee. Probate Code 10954(b)(4). 4) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] 5) Receipts for any preliminary distribution must be on file prior to the approval of final distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) c.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AURA P. HUCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies of the petition or proof of service have been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proposed order. Proposed orders should not be E-filed. [LRSF 14.7 A.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/3/21. (Probate Code Section 17200 deals with trusts. This also does not appear to be a petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850. It appears to be a petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 12251.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/2021. 1) Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. 2) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. 3) The I&A alleges under #4 by order of the court dated (specify) 12/23/2019 [No Sch 2 Items]. Please explain the order of the court dated 12/23/2019 appointed Karen Ann Simons as referee. 4) There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. 5) Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. 6) Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. 7) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 8) Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. 9) The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. 10) The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAHUL BHIDE LIVING TRUST, TRUST CREATED AND EXECUTED NOVEMBER 13, 2012 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Ownership Of Trust Property And Authorize Conveyance
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/23/21. no later than noon on 8/16/21, objector must file amended objections that address only the assets in the amended petition (bank accounts). the amended objections must not contain any reference to the real property, which is no longer at issue in the amended petition. in addition, by that same deadline, petitioner must file authority for this court to confirm an IRA and 401k plan as trust assets, given the langauge quoted in paragraph 4 of the supplement filed 5/18/21, wherein the trustor specifically exempts IRA and tax deferred accounts. finally, this court does not order that a trust is valid when signing an order on an 850 petition. that relief is only obtained by filing a petition specifically seeking to confirm a trust is valid. in addition, this court does not include an omnibus clause in an order on an 850 petition as each asset must be described with specificity and notice given to protect any interest in the newly discovered asset. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK JOSEPH GOLDSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The will is not self-proving. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. Attachments 7a and 7b do not show the Decedent's interest in each property presumably his 100% interest in the six pieces of separate property (one held as "a married man" (?)), but what portion of the property held in joint tenancy listed on Attachment 7b and when property held in joint tenancy passes by operation of law to the surviving joint tenant? (It would have been helpful if Attachment 7 had referenced exhibit numbers and the attached deeds had been numbered accordingly, as the common addresses are not shown on the deeds so that the properties listed on Attachment 7 had to be cross-referenced with the APNs on Attachments 7a and 7b and then to the attached deeds.) Courtesy copies of papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/23/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WOON BING CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Papers filed in response to the tentative ruling for the 7/12/21 hearing have not yet been filed. The copy of the as-yet unfiled "Supplemental Declaration" was skimmed as a one-time courtesy. Petitioner simply claims that the funds were from income from both the decedent's and his employment during marriage but does not address why the accounts were held in the decedent's name alone and does not address whether the couple owned any separate property prior to marriage or acquired any assets through inheritance before or during their marriage. The proposed order lists "all funds" held in the five bank accounts on both Attachment 7a and 7b, not just the ½ community property interest that the surviving spouse seeks to have confirmed as passing to him or belonging to him. And if all funds were indeed community property, order 5a corresponds to Attachment 7a of the petition, and order 7a corresponds with item 7b of the petition. Courtesy copies must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/23/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW ALVIN BURLEIGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether the 6124 presumption applies and whether it has been rebutted. 2) Court to determine whether more needs to be done to locate heirs.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WING H. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/2021 1) Petitioner has not filed the required proof of publication. (Probate Code 8120 et seq) 2) Declination of Mamie Louie to Serve as Executor; Nomination of Cory Louie includes text written in a foreign language. Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15]. 3) Petitioner filed a Final I&A prior to being appointed. The Final I&A does not have any required attachments. (Probate Code 8800 et seq.) 4) The bank statements attached to the petition show the accounts titled as Wing Louie POD Cory Louie. Please explain if the accounts are pay on death accounts and who the beneficiary on the account is. If all the alleged assets are pay on death accounts, please explain why a petition for probate has been filed. 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order for probate (DE-140) to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEAN HANEY REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED JUNE 15, 2015
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. the court may wish to discuss whether the trust should be under court supervision for the first year. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUNG-DOW CHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/23/21 to allow time to address the following: no proof of service of the notice of administration on file or in the efiling queue; no proof of publication on file or in the efiling queue; no proposed order delivered to the examiner in room 202 as required by local rules; no confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form on file (please note, this form must have a caption page and cannot be efiled); judicial council form DE-142 is a mandatory form for waivers of bond as of 7/1/2017, so new waivers on this form must be signed and filed if bond is to be waived; a supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner must be filed that identifies the persons in section 8 of the petition by their relationship to the decedent. deadline for curing all issues in order to avoid an automatic continuance is noon on 8/16/21. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IGNACIO JIMENEZ PLASCENCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/2021. Issues: 1) Petitioner has not filed the original Will. [Probate Code 8200]. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
